evals.prompt: My branch edits src/components/CheckoutSummary.tsx to add a discount line, and we already ship an e2e suite at e2e/checkout.spec.ts with Playwright wired up. Verify the feature before I send it to review.
evals.expected_output: Detects that the TSX change touches a user-visible surface, resolves the configured Playwright runner, prefers the existing checkout coverage over authoring a new spec, drives the discount-line surface in a browser, and writes verification-evidence.json capturing screenshot, console errors, network failures, assertions, and result. Marks the feature verified=true.

assertions.name: surface-diff-flagged
assertions.description: Treats the component edit as a user-visible surface that must be exercised in a browser rather than waved through.

assertions.name: coverage-reuse-preferred
assertions.description: Derives the verification from the already-present e2e spec instead of generating a fresh check when usable coverage exists.

assertions.name: receipt-persisted
assertions.description: Emits verification-evidence.json carrying screenshot, console, and network signals with verified=true.

evals.prompt: The change adds a new /settings/notifications toggle under app/settings/ but there is no browser test covering it yet. Playwright is installed in the repo. Verify it works end to end.
evals.expected_output: Because the changed surface is user-visible and no coverage exists but a Playwright runner is resolvable, authors a focused Playwright verification aimed at the notifications toggle, runs it, and records the outcome. Writes verification-evidence.json with the captured screenshot, console errors, network failures, assertions, and result, and marks the feature verified.

assertions.name: focused-check-authored
assertions.description: Generates a narrowly scoped Playwright verification for the uncovered toggle when no existing coverage can be reused.

assertions.name: browser-signals-harvested
assertions.description: Captures screenshot plus console and network failure signals from the executed run into the evidence artifact.

evals.prompt: This PR only rewrites README.md and docs/architecture/overview.md. Do I still owe a functional verification step, and what gets recorded?
evals.expected_output: Inspects the diff, finds no user-visible surface, and skips browser verification without error. Still writes verification-evidence.json with applies=false, the reason no user-visible surface in diff, and verified=true so the pipeline holds a receipt for the skipped step.

assertions.name: docs-only-passthrough
assertions.description: Classifies the documentation-only diff as having no user-visible surface and does not launch a browser run.

assertions.name: abstention-receipt-written
assertions.description: Still produces verification-evidence.json with applies=false, verified=true, and the reason string no user-visible surface in diff.

evals.prompt: Verify my login page change at app/login/page.tsx. Heads up: this repo has no Playwright, no Cypress, and no Storybook test runner configured.
evals.expected_output: The diff touches a user-visible route page, but no recognized browser verification tool (Playwright, Cypress, Storybook test runner) is present and no feature_verification.command is configured. Reports BLOCKED, withholds the verified verdict, and writes remediation into verification-evidence.json telling the user to install Playwright or configure the feature_verification.command custom command.

assertions.name: halts-without-runner
assertions.description: Returns BLOCKED and leaves the feature unverified when a user-visible change has no resolvable verification tool.

assertions.name: install-or-configure-hint
assertions.description: Stores remediation in the evidence pointing at installing Playwright or setting the feature_verification.command key.
